decks.notes: The deck focuses on utilizing artifacts to control the board and disrupt the opponent's strategy. By playing cards like Karn, the Great Creator and Trinisphere, the deck aims to slow down the opponent and lock them out of the game. Planar Nexus and Urza's Saga help generate extra value, while cards like Kozilek's Command and Emrakul, the Aeons Torn provide powerful threats. The deck also includes mana ramp with Urza's Tower, Urza's Workshop, and Grim Monolith to accelerate its game plan. Overall, the deck aims to establish a "prison" for the opponent through a combination of artifacts and control elements.

The deck focuses on quickly putting powerful creatures like Griselbrand and Emrakul, the Aeons Torn into play by using cards like Show and Tell and Sneak Attack. It also utilizes cards like Omniscience and Atraxa, Grand Unifier to gain additional advantage and disrupt opponents' strategies. The deck aims to win by overwhelming the opponent with big creatures and powerful spells before they can establish their own game plan. It also includes counterspells like Flusterstorm and Force of Will to protect its key combo pieces and ensure a successful setup.

The key concept of this deck is to quickly assemble the combo pieces of Aluren, Ancient Tomb, Show and Tell, Omniscience, and Acererak the Archlich in order to cheat powerful creatures like Emrakul, the Aeons Torn and Atraxa, Grand Unifier into play without paying their mana costs. This allows the player to overwhelm their opponent with big threats and quickly close out the game. The deck also includes disruption spells like Force of Will and Veil of Summer to protect the combo and ensure its success.

This deck focuses on deploying threats quickly while disrupting the opponent's game plan through discard spells like Thoughtseize and counterspells like Force of Will. The main goal is to tempo the opponent by utilizing powerful creatures like Kaito, Bane of Nightmares and Murktide Regent while maintaining card advantage with Tamiyo, Inquisitive Student. The mana denial strategy with Wasteland and disruptive creatures like Orcish Bowmasters further hinders the opponent's ability to stabilize, allowing the deck to close out games efficiently.

The deck focuses on quickly assembling a combo that allows the player to win the game in one explosive turn. By using cards like Lotus Petal and Simian Spirit Guide to generate mana, along with spells like Dark Ritual and Chrome Mox, the deck aims to play a series of powerful spells like Balustrade Spy and Boggart Trawler to mill the entire deck and win with a Thassa's Oracle trigger. The inclusion of cards like Pact of Negation and Chancellor of the Annex help protect the combo from disruption, making it a fast and resilient deck in the Legacy format.

The deck is built around the concept of quickly milling through the entire deck to either win the game by comboing off with Thassa's Oracle or Dread Return, or by overwhelming the opponent with creatures like Poxwalkers and Boggart Trawler. It utilizes cards like Simian Spirit Guide and Elvish Spirit Guide to accelerate mana production, while also including disruption spells like Thoughtseize and Pact of Negation to protect the combo. The deck relies on a high-risk, high-reward strategy of going all-in on its combo as quickly as possible to win the game before the opponent can respond.

The deck focuses on quickly bringing out powerful creatures like Emrakul, the Aeons Torn and Archon of Cruelty by using cards like Show and Tell and Sneak Attack. It also includes a variety of counterspells and card draw to control the game and find the combo pieces needed to win. The deck aims to disrupt the opponent's plan while rapidly assembling its own win condition through cheating out big creatures onto the battlefield. It relies on speed and surprise to overwhelm the opponent and secure victory.

The deck focuses on utilizing artifact cards like Urza's Saga, Karn, the Great Creator, and Planar Nexus to control the board and lock down opponents. By using powerful artifacts such as The One Ring, Trinisphere, and Candelabra of Tawnos, the deck aims to limit the opponent's options and disrupt their game plan. Cards like Grim Monolith and Urza's Workshop help generate mana to cast powerful threats like Emrakul, the Aeons Torn and Ugin, the Eye of the Storms. Overall, the deck aims to establish a prison-like control strategy using artifacts to overwhelm and outlast the opponent.

This deck focuses on creating a powerful combo using cards like Goblin Engineer, Fable of the Mirror-Breaker, and Goblin Welder to quickly assemble pieces that can lead to explosive plays. It utilizes artifacts and fast mana sources like Mox Opal and Ancient Tomb to ramp up and set up the combo. The inclusion of cards like Ensnaring Bridge and Pyroblast helps to control the board while pushing towards victory. The deck also has some unique tools like Sewer-veillance Cam and Chaos Defiler to add versatility and surprise elements to its strategy.

The deck focuses on generating a high storm count to cast powerful spells like Ad Nauseam and Tendrils of Agony to win the game quickly. It utilizes cards like Dark Ritual, Lion's Eye Diamond, and Lotus Petal to generate mana quickly and efficiently. Wishclaw Talisman and Infernal Tutor are key in finding the necessary pieces to execute the combo. Urza's Saga provides additional value and tutoring capabilities. The deck also includes disruption in the form of cards like Thoughtseize and Duress to protect the combo and disrupt the opponent's game plan.

The deck seeks to establish a lock on the opponent through a combination of artifact-based control elements. It aims to disrupt the opponent's game plan by utilizing cards like Trinisphere and Karn, the Great Creator, while also utilizing powerful artifacts like Candelabra of Tawnos and Grim Monolith for mana acceleration. The deck ultimately looks to control the game and seal the victory by using powerful artifacts like Emrakul, the Aeons Torn and Ugin, the Eye of the Storms.

This deck focuses on creating an aggressive board presence by playing powerful creatures like Magus of the Moon and Pyrogoyf, supported by disruptive cards like Chalice of the Void. It also aims to quickly ramp into big threats using mana acceleration cards like Simian Spirit Guide and Ancient Tomb. The deck utilizes red mana symbols extensively, enabling cards like Sundering Eruption and Fury to provide additional removal and reach. The goal is to overwhelm the opponent with a combination of creature pressure and impactful spells, ultimately securing victory through aggressive tactics.

The deck focuses on using artifacts to control the game and create a prison-like environment for the opponent. By utilizing cards like Karn, the Great Creator, Trinisphere, and Planar Nexus, the deck aims to limit the opponent's options and prevent them from executing their game plan effectively. With cards like Urza's Tower, Urza's Mine, and Urza's Power Plant, the deck can generate a significant amount of mana to cast powerful threats like Ugin, Eye of the Storms and Emrakul, the Aeons Torn. Additionally, cards like Manifold Key and Voltaic Key can be used to untap artifacts for additional value.

This deck focuses on disrupting opponent's strategies while efficiently deploying aggressive threats. It utilizes cards like Swords to Plowshares, Thoughtseize, and Skyclave Apparition to control the board, while also leveraging Aether Vial and Stoneforge Mystic to quickly deploy powerful creatures like Phelia, Exuberant Shepherd and Solitude. The inclusion of Yorion, Sky Nomad as a companion allows for additional flexibility and value generation. The deck aims to pressure opponents while disrupting their game plan, ultimately overwhelming them with a combination of aggressive creatures and disruptive elements.

The main theme of this deck is to disrupt the opponent's strategies while applying pressure with aggressive creatures. By combining efficient removal spells like Fatal Push and Snuff Out with counter spells like Daze and Force of Will, this deck aims to control the game tempo. The deck also utilizes cards like Brainstorm and Ponder for card selection and digging for key threats. With a mix of disruptive spells and powerful creatures like Murktide Regent and Barrowgoyf, this deck aims to maintain control over the game while consistently applying pressure to the opponent.

The main concept of this Combo - Oops, All Spells! deck is to quickly and efficiently fill the graveyard with creatures and then win the game by getting Thassa's Oracle onto the battlefield with little to no resistance from the opponent. The deck leverages cards like Cabal Therapy, Undercity Informer, and Balustrade Spy to quickly dump creatures into the graveyard, while using various spells like Dark Ritual and Lotus Petal to generate mana and cast Thassa's Oracle to win the game. The deck aims to be fast and consistent in executing its game plan.
